rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEARNEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 14. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Manwill¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Kearney was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation in debir that was disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Manwill¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEARNEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 14. Defendant Oakfabco Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the gaskets and refractory material removed from Kewanee boilers were original factory parts. Defendant¿s separate statement did not include sufficient facts to show that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that none of the original factory parts of the Kewanee boilers that Mr. Kearney was exposed to were asbestos-free. Moreover, Mr. Kasparian¿s deposition testimony (submitted by plaintiffs) creates a triable issue whether those parts contained asbestos. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FABIAN CARCALLAS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 15. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carcallas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Carcallas¿ deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FABIAN CARCALLAS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 15. Defendant Graybar Electric Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carcallas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Carcallas¿ deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FABIAN CARCALLAS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 15. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 3 (negligence/strict liability and punitive damages) and granted as to issue 2 (false representation). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carcallas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Carcallas¿ deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on this issue. There are triable issues whether defendant acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos-containing products. On plaintiffs¿ claim for false representation, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant ever made any representation. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE GLEASON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Star Machine 7 Tool Co. For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Star Machine & Tool Co.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted and plaintiff¿s request for leave to amend is denied. A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). Neither the complaint nor the proposed amended complaint alleges that defendant sold an asbestos-containing product or that it had any control over the design and manufacture of the asbestos-containing brake linings used in conjunction with its machine. The machines are analogous to the stove in Garman and the acid in Blackwell v. Phelps Dodge Corp. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 372. As in these cases, the plaintiff¿s injury allegedly would not have occurred but for the defendant¿s product, but it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH GARZA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Order Partially Amending Judgment, To Include Additional Name Of Mazarin Inc., A Corporation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Plaintiffs¿ motion for order partially amending judgment to include additional name of Mazarin Inc. is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown that Mazarin is the alter ego or successor of defendant or that Mazarin received any transfers from defendant, let alone any fraudulent ones, or that Mazarin controlled the litigation up to the date of the judgment or that Mazarin was properly served with this motion. While the first five pages of plaintiffs¿ reply memorandum can be construed as a motion to strike defendant¿s opposition papers, even if so construed and granted, the motion to add Mazarin to the judgment would still be denied due to the failure of plaintiffs to establish any basis to add Mazarin. = (220/HEK)
